name: E2open Supply Chain Management API
what_it_does: E2open REST API for connected supply chain management platform. Enables AI agents to manage supply chain visibility and multi-tier tracking, handle demand sensing and forecasting workflows, access supplier collaboration and purchase order management, retrieve trade compliance and customs documentation data, manage logistics and transportation optimization, handle inventory optimization and replenishment, access supply chain risk management data, retrieve channel data management and sell-through analytics, manage contract manufacturing and outsourced logistics, and integrate supply chain data across ERP, TMS, and WMS systems.
use_cases: Managing supply chain visibility and multi-tier tracking from supply chain intelligence agents
Handling demand forecasting and supplier collaboration from procurement automation agents
Retrieving trade compliance and customs data from global trade agents
Integrating supply chain data with ERP and TMS from enterprise supply chain agents
not_for: Simple procurement without multi-tier supply chain complexity
Domestic-only operations without global trade compliance needs
Small businesses without enterprise supply chain platform investment
best_when: A global enterprise using E2open wants AI agents to automate supply chain visibility, demand planning, supplier collaboration, trade compliance, logistics optimization, and cross-tier data integration.
avoid_when: OPERATIONAL RISK: Supply chain data drives real procurement and logistics decisions — automated PO issuance and logistics execution affects financial commitments. Trade compliance automation must be accurate — customs errors create shipment delays and penalties. Multi-tier supplier data is confidential business information.

auth.notes: E2open uses OAuth 2.0 for API access. Enterprise customer and partner credentials with supply chain, visibility, and compliance scopes. Developer portal with API documentation. Webhooks for supply chain event notifications. Platform built from multiple acquisitions (Logility, Amber Road, INTTRA).

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — enterprise SCM subscription required
pricing.paid_tiers: Module and transaction volume enterprise pricing; annual contracts
pricing.notes: Austin, Texas. NASDAQ: ETWO (taken private 2023 by Francisco Partners). Founded 2000. Major connected supply chain platform built through acquisitions. $600M+ revenue. Used by Toyota, Lenovo, and HP. Acquired Logility (2022), Amber Road (trade compliance), INTTRA (ocean freight). Competes with Kinaxis and Blue Yonder.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; enterprise SLA

known_agent_gotchas: OPERATIONAL RISK: Automated PO issuance and logistics execution creates financial commitments — approval controls required
Trade compliance errors delay shipments and create customs penalties — accuracy requirements are strict
No public MCP server — REST API with developer portal
Platform built from acquisitions — API consistency across Logility, Amber Road, INTTRA may vary
Francisco Partners take-private (2023) — verify API roadmap stability post-acquisition
Multi-tier supplier data sharing requires supplier consent and data governance agreements
agent_readiness.security_notes: Supply chain and trade compliance data. SOC2, ISO27001, C-TPAT. OAuth2. Multi-region. Supplier data confidentiality and trade data sensitivity.

security_score_components.security_notes: Supply chain and global trade data. SOC2, ISO27001, C-TPAT. OAuth2. Multi-region. Supplier and customs data sensitivity.
